project_title: The Effects of Nutrition on Depression and Anxiety in Adults
abstract: Depression and anxiety are the leading mental health disorders in the world and increasing evidence supports that nutrition significantly influences the cause, severity and management of mental health conditions. The field of nutrition explores how diet patterns, inflammation potential and gut health impact mental health, specifically depression and anxiety. This literature review examines the relationship between nutrition and its effects on depression and anxiety in adults. Evidence from the studies that suggests improved dietary patterns, especially those that follow Mediterranean style practices, anti-inflammatory diet practices and probiotic intervention can reduce depression and anxiety symptoms.
